project.name: MTM Receiving Application
notes: WinUI 3 desktop app with MVVM, CQRS, and database-backed workflows.
Use this catalog as the single editable source for feature names and suggested code entry points.

features.name: Receiving Workflow
features.summary: Primary receiving flow for queueing, editing, and archiving receiving label data.

features.name: Bulk Inventory Processing
features.summary: Bulk inventory workflows, automations, and reconciliation logic.

features.name: Reporting Dashboard
features.summary: Report generation, filtering, and user-facing export flows.

features.name: Dunnage Management
features.summary: Dunnage setup, lookup, and transaction tracking workflows.

features.name: Volvo Integration
features.summary: Volvo-specific intake, validation, and integration flows.

forms.title: UI Change Request
forms.summary: Capture UI layout, content, visual hierarchy, and interaction changes.
whenToUse: Use this when the screen already exists and the user wants it changed.
Use this when labels, grouping, layout, navigation, or interaction details need to change.
Do not use this if the main problem is incorrect business logic or a bug with no UI design intent.
beforeYouStart: Know the screen name, workflow step, or control names if possible.
Write down what feels wrong today and what the user wants instead.
List any parts of the workflow that must stay exactly the same.
endUserTips: Use plain language first and exact control names second.
Describe the result the user should see, not the code you think should change.
If copy matters, paste the exact text you want shown.

fieldGroups.title: Request Summary
fieldGroups.description: State what the user wants changed and who the UI serves.

fields.label: Short request title
fields.placeholder: Receiving workflow header cleanup

fields.label: What should change
fields.placeholder: Describe the current UI issue and the desired end state.

fields.label: What success looks like
fields.placeholder: Describe what the user should be able to do more easily after the change.

fieldGroups.title: Visual And Interaction Detail
fieldGroups.description: Capture layout, content, and behavior detail that Copilot should preserve.

fields.label: Affected screens or controls
fields.placeholder: One screen or control per line

fields.label: Layout and spacing notes
fields.placeholder: Describe position, grouping, spacing, sizing, or responsive expectations.

fields.label: Interaction changes
fields.placeholder: Describe button behavior, command changes, focus order, validation, or navigation changes.

fields.label: Text or copy changes
fields.placeholder: Provide any labels, button text, tooltips, or status messages that should change.

fields.label: Accessibility or usability constraints
fields.placeholder: Keyboard navigation, contrast, larger hit targets, reduced confusion, or user training needs.

fields.label: Specific files to review first
fields.placeholder: One path per line

fields.label: What must not change
fields.placeholder: Business logic, data flow, command names, test behavior, or existing integrations that should remain intact.

fields.label: Acceptance criteria
fields.placeholder: One acceptance criterion per line

forms.title: Debugging Request
forms.summary: Capture symptoms, repro steps, expected behavior, and evidence.
whenToUse: Use this when something is broken, inconsistent, or failing unexpectedly.
Use this when you can describe what happens and what should have happened instead.
Do not use this for a pure redesign or cleanup request.
beforeYouStart: Reproduce the problem at least once if possible.
Capture exact error text, logs, or screenshots if available.
Write the shortest reliable repro steps you can.
endUserTips: Facts matter more than guesses.
If the issue is intermittent, say when it tends to happen.
Paste exact messages instead of paraphrasing them.

fields.label: Short bug title
fields.placeholder: Receiving load list does not refresh after clear

fields.label: Observed behavior
fields.placeholder: Describe the failure exactly as the user sees it.

fields.label: Expected behavior
fields.placeholder: Describe the expected result.

fields.label: Reproduction steps
fields.placeholder: One step per line

fields.label: How often it happens
fields.placeholder: Always, intermittent, only after navigation, only in Debug, and so on.

fields.label: Environment detail
fields.placeholder: User role, machine, branch, config, test data, or timing conditions.

fields.label: Error messages or visible warnings
fields.placeholder: Paste exact messages if available.

fields.label: Relevant logs, traces, or breakpoints
fields.placeholder: Paste log snippets, stack traces, or debugger observations.

fields.label: Suspected cause
fields.placeholder: If you have a theory, describe it. If not, say Unknown.

fields.label: Likely files or layers
fields.placeholder: One path or component per line

fields.label: How to verify the fix
fields.placeholder: One verification step per line

forms.title: Feature Logic Correction
forms.summary: Capture where feature behavior does not match the intended workflow or rules.
whenToUse: Use this when the feature runs but behaves incorrectly according to user intent or business rules.
Use this when the UI may be fine but the workflow decisions are wrong.
Do not use this for styling-only requests.
beforeYouStart: Describe the intended workflow in plain language before describing the bug.
List the rules the feature should follow.
Identify any inputs or data conditions that change the expected result.
endUserTips: Focus on intent versus actual behavior.
Give examples of good and bad outcomes.
List what must keep working after the correction.

fields.label: What the feature is supposed to do
fields.placeholder: Describe the intended business behavior in plain language.

fields.label: What it does today
fields.placeholder: Describe the current behavior.

fields.label: Business rules being violated
fields.placeholder: State any rule or assumption the current logic breaks.

fields.label: Input conditions that matter
fields.placeholder: One condition per line

fields.label: Related data or database expectations
fields.placeholder: Describe fields, stored procedures, or external data involved.

fields.label: Layers or services involved
fields.placeholder: ViewModel, Service, DAO, stored procedure, and so on.

fields.label: Constraints to preserve
fields.placeholder: Performance, architecture, database rules, or backwards compatibility constraints.

fields.label: Desired rule set
fields.placeholder: One rule per line

fields.label: Key scenarios to validate
fields.placeholder: One scenario per line

fields.label: What must not regress
fields.placeholder: List related flows that must remain stable.

forms.title: Improvement Refactor Request
forms.summary: Capture maintainability, clarity, structure, and performance-driven refactors.
whenToUse: Use this when the behavior is acceptable but the code is hard to maintain, understand, or extend.
Use this for cleanup, decomposition, duplication removal, or clearer structure.
Do not use this when the main issue is a production bug or incorrect user behavior.
beforeYouStart: Identify the biggest pain points for future maintenance.
Decide how broad the refactor is allowed to be.
List any sensitive areas that must stay stable.
endUserTips: Explain why the current code is difficult to work with.
Keep the scope realistic.
Call out whether behavior must remain identical.

fields.label: Why this refactor is needed
fields.placeholder: State the maintainability, readability, duplication, or complexity problem.

fields.label: Current pain points
fields.placeholder: One pain point per line

fields.label: Preferred end state
fields.placeholder: Describe the desired code shape, structure, or ownership boundary.

fields.label: Files or types likely involved
fields.placeholder: One file or type per line

fields.label: Architecture notes
fields.placeholder: Mention MVVM boundaries, service seams, DAO rules, or dependency concerns.

fields.label: Tests or validation needed
fields.placeholder: One validation item per line

fields.label: Success criteria
fields.placeholder: One success criterion per line

forms.title: Logging Refactor Request
forms.summary: Capture logging additions, removals, noise reduction, or structure changes.
whenToUse: Use this when log output is missing, too noisy, inconsistent, or exposing the wrong details.
Use this when the main change is about observability rather than workflow behavior.
Do not use this for general refactors unless logging is the main objective.
beforeYouStart: Know whether you want more logs, fewer logs, or better-structured logs.
List which events matter and which logs are noise.
Note any sensitive data that must never be logged.
endUserTips: State whether the change is for debugging, support, compliance, or performance.
Be explicit about which logs should stay or go.
If severity matters, explain what should count as an error versus a warning.

fields.label: Why logging should change
fields.placeholder: State the debugging, audit, noise, or compliance reason.

fields.label: Current logging issue
fields.placeholder: Too noisy, too sparse, inconsistent, missing correlation, wrong severity, and so on.

fields.label: Desired logging outcome
fields.placeholder: Describe the target signal quality, structure, and severity usage.

fields.label: Events or transitions that should be logged
fields.placeholder: One event per line

fields.label: Logs to remove or suppress
fields.placeholder: One log pattern per line

fields.label: Severity rules
fields.placeholder: Describe what should be Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, or Error.

fields.label: Sensitive data handling
fields.placeholder: Describe what must not be logged.

fields.label: Relevant files or services
fields.placeholder: One path or type per line

fields.label: How to validate the logging change
fields.placeholder: One validation step per line

forms.title: Code Review Request
forms.summary: Capture a review request focused on defects, regressions, risks, architecture violations, and missing tests.
whenToUse: Use this when you want Copilot to review a feature, module, or set of changes for problems.
Use this when the main goal is finding risks rather than implementing a requested change.
Use this for review of planned or completed work.
beforeYouStart: Name the module, feature, or files being reviewed.
State whether this is a full review or a focused review.
List any specific concerns like security, regressions, or missing tests.
endUserTips: Point to the exact area you care about if the codebase is large.
Say whether you want critical defects only or a full review.
Mention any recent changes or known unstable areas.

fields.label: Short review title
fields.placeholder: Review receiving clear-to-history changes

fields.label: Target files or areas
fields.placeholder: One file, folder, or type per line

fields.label: What should the review focus on
fields.placeholder: Regressions, architecture violations, data risks, missing tests, performance, security, and so on.

fields.label: Specific concerns
fields.placeholder: One concern per line

fields.label: Recent changes or background
fields.placeholder: Describe recent edits, PR intent, or unstable areas.

fields.label: Expected review output
fields.placeholder: Example: findings first, severity ordered, file references, testing gaps.

forms.title: Database Issue Request
forms.summary: Capture stored procedure, SQL, schema, connection, or read-model issues that need investigation or correction.
whenToUse: Use this when the issue is in stored procedures, database mappings, query results, or schema alignment.
Use this when the code problem cannot be understood without database context.
Do not use this for purely visual UI changes.
beforeYouStart: Identify whether the issue is MySQL or Infor Visual SQL Server.
Name the stored procedure, table, query, or DAO if known.
Capture any mismatched data, SQL errors, or incorrect result shapes.
endUserTips: Paste exact SQL or stored procedure names when available.
State whether the failure is read, write, schema, or data-shape related.
Mention any constraints such as read-only Visual access or stored-procedure-only rules.

fields.label: Short database issue title
fields.placeholder: sp_Receiving_LabelData_Insert missing field mapping

fields.label: Affected procedures, tables, queries, or DAOs
fields.placeholder: One object per line

fields.label: Problem summary
fields.placeholder: Describe the mismatch, failure, or incorrect data behavior.

fields.label: SQL errors, mismatched data, or examples
fields.placeholder: Paste exact messages, row examples, or result differences.

fields.label: Important database constraints
fields.placeholder: Stored procedures only, read-only Visual DB, no schema changes, and so on.

fields.label: How to validate the database fix
fields.placeholder: One validation step per line

forms.title: Test Generation Request
forms.summary: Capture which code needs tests, the intended scope, and what behavior the tests must prove.
whenToUse: Use this when you want Copilot to add or expand tests for a file, class, or module.
Use this when the main output is tests, not production code changes.
Use this for unit or integration test requests.
beforeYouStart: Know which code needs coverage.
Decide whether you want unit, integration, or mixed tests.
List critical behaviors, edge cases, and dependencies that should be mocked or left real.
endUserTips: State the test scope clearly.
Mention whether the target uses DAOs, services, or UI-bound classes.
List the behaviors that matter more than coverage percentage.

fields.label: Short test request title
fields.placeholder: Add ViewModel tests for receiving workflow

fields.label: Files, classes, or modules to test
fields.placeholder: One target per line

fields.label: What these tests must prove
fields.placeholder: Describe core behaviors, edge cases, and failure paths.

fields.label: Dependencies to mock or keep real
fields.placeholder: Describe services, DAOs, database access, or framework dependencies.

fields.label: Required scenarios
fields.placeholder: One scenario per line

fields.label: Testing constraints
fields.placeholder: Naming patterns, no database access, use FluentAssertions, and so on.

forms.title: Documentation Change Request
forms.summary: Capture missing, outdated, or newly required documentation and the audience it must serve.
whenToUse: Use this when documentation needs to be created, corrected, or expanded.
Use this when the user goal is clearer guidance for developers, operators, or end users.
Use this when code changes require synchronized docs.
beforeYouStart: Know which audience the document is for.
List the source files, modules, or workflows the docs must reflect.
State whether the problem is missing docs, stale docs, or poor structure.
endUserTips: Describe who will read the documentation.
Call out sections that are wrong or incomplete today.
If the docs must match code exactly, say so explicitly.

fields.label: Short documentation request title
fields.placeholder: Update receiving workflow support guide

fields.label: What is wrong or missing
fields.placeholder: Describe the missing, stale, or confusing documentation.

fields.label: Desired documentation outcome
fields.placeholder: Describe what the finished documentation should help the reader do.

fields.label: Source files or modules to inspect
fields.placeholder: One path or module per line

fields.label: Sections or topics to include
fields.placeholder: One topic per line

fields.label: How to verify the documentation
fields.placeholder: One validation step per line

forms.title: UI Mockup Request
forms.summary: Capture enough visual and workflow detail for Copilot to generate a mockup artifact for a screen or flow.
whenToUse: Use this when the user wants a new screen concept or a visual mockup before implementation.
Use this when a normal UI change request is not enough because the screen is still exploratory.
Do not use this when the request is only for code cleanup.
beforeYouStart: Know the screen purpose and main user actions.
List required fields, actions, and sections.
Describe any layout or visual style constraints that matter.
endUserTips: Describe the screen as if explaining it to a designer.
Prioritize the most important user actions.
Mention desktop assumptions if the screen is WinUI-specific.

fields.label: Short mockup title
fields.placeholder: Receiving discrepancy entry screen

fields.label: Screen purpose
fields.placeholder: Describe what this screen helps the user do.

fields.label: Primary user actions
fields.placeholder: One action per line

fields.label: Required sections or cards
fields.placeholder: One section per line

fields.label: Important fields or controls
fields.placeholder: One field or control per line

fields.label: Visual or layout notes
fields.placeholder: Describe hierarchy, spacing, panel layout, tables, buttons, and status areas.

fields.label: Constraints to preserve
fields.placeholder: Window size, desktop assumptions, branding, accessibility, or workflow constraints.
